_note: SAMPLE DATA for layout preview only. Replace with real member pitches, then set sampleData to false. Each pitch needs: ticker, pitcher (first name + last initial), date (YYYY-MM-DD), thesisSummary, keyRisks, and status: one of 'in discussion', 'voting soon', 'approved', 'declined'. This board is display-only; voting happens in member meetings for now.

pitches.thesisSummary: The club already agrees AI compute demand is real; the debate is the price. Ethan argues data-center revenue can stay ahead of even aggressive estimates for at least two more years.
pitches.keyRisks: Valuation leaves little room for error; customers are concentrated among a few big tech buyers who are designing their own chips; the semiconductor cycle always turns eventually.

pitches.thesisSummary: A turnaround pitch: the brand is intact, inventory is finally clean, and a new product cycle is starting while the stock trades near multi-year lows.
pitches.keyRisks: Turnarounds usually take longer than expected; running brands like On and Hoka keep taking share; a weak consumer cuts back on sneakers early.

pitches.thesisSummary: Approved 12 votes to 3 and executed on May 21; see current holdings. Sofia's pitch: search cash flows are underpriced relative to the AI and cloud upside they fund.
pitches.keyRisks: AI chat products could erode search query volume over time; regulators are circling the advertising business on two continents.

pitches.thesisSummary: A momentum pitch built around meme-cycle attention. The club declined it: excitement isn't a thesis, and the pitch had no valuation logic. It's now our reference example of what a pitch needs before it can reach a vote.
pitches.keyRisks: The pitch itself named none, which was part of the problem.
